_doc: TSBC power-mode rules. The controller (tsbc-power.py, hourly launchd) computes mode and writes .tsbc-power.json; bench.py reads it to cap concurrency + gate heavy tasks. LOW during any heavy-hitter's LIVE sprint window or any ad-hoc sprint; NORMAL otherwise.
heavyHitterBasis: token consumption + process-time over 7d (NOT run count): Capital 332.8Mtok (quant, ~267K/run), Media 200.3Mtok (render+output), Books 183.9Mtok (most gen output). Excluded: TSMC (always-on baseline), KISS 114M / Recruitment 92M / DP 91M (lighter). Windows read LIVE from DB so ad-hoc extensions are caught automatically.
maxWorkersNote: normal=2 (conservative: the live fleet + TSMC run 24/7 and heavy fan-out has OOM'd this Mac; bump to 3 if it proves stable). low=1 (zero added pressure).

refresh.note: each suite is re-tested ~monthly; due-day = stable-hash(suite) % staggerOverDays, so suites refresh on different days and never all re-enter testing at once.

sleepDream.note: TSBC is 24/7, not sprint-bound, so it needs a rhythm. DREAM (daily, just after the 04:00 ramp) = self-improvement instead of raw testing: analyse the ledger, refine suites, re-tag skills, fold in real fleet data. SLEEP (weekly, Sun 04-08) = full idle + deep dream. Execution lands with the Drillmaster operator; the controller emits the flags now.
